name: Revenant
description: A revenant is an animated corpse that is believed to return from the grave to terrorize the living. Unlike ghosts or specters, revenants are typically depicted as physically reanimated bodies, retaining their corporeal form, albeit often in a decaying or corrupted state. The belief in revenants is widespread in European folklore, particularly in Slavic and Germanic traditions. They are often driven by vengeance, unresolved issues from their former lives, or a malevolent spirit that has taken possession of the deceased body. Revenants are typically stronger and more resilient than ordinary humans, possessing supernatural strength and an immunity to conventional weapons. The specific methods for creating and destroying a revenant vary depending on the local folklore. However, common themes involve improper burial rites, unfulfilled promises, or the presence of dark magic. The fear of revenants reflects anxieties about death, the unknown, and the potential for the dead to disrupt the lives of the living. In some tales, a revenant might seek justice or resolution, while in others, it is simply a force of chaos and destruction.
subtitle: An animated corpse returned from the grave.
appearance: A reanimated corpse, often decaying or bloated, with pale skin and sunken eyes. May exhibit signs of trauma or decomposition.
habitat: Graveyards, burial grounds, abandoned buildings, or any location associated with their death.
behavior: Driven by vengeance, unresolved issues, or a malevolent spirit. Typically hostile and aggressive towards the living, seeking to cause harm or spread fear.

keyMyths.title: The Revenant of Croglin Grange
keyMyths.description: A classic English ghost story recounts a revenant that terrorized the residents of Croglin Grange, attacking a young woman and drinking her blood. The creature was eventually tracked down to its grave and destroyed by a group of men using axes and fire.

keyMyths.title: The Draugr of Norse Sagas
keyMyths.description: While not precisely revenants in the same vein as European folklore, the Draugr of Norse sagas share similarities. These undead beings possess superhuman strength and often guard their burial mounds, attacking anyone who dares to disturb their rest. They are formidable opponents, requiring specific methods to defeat.

keyMyths.title: Slavic Vampires and Revenants
keyMyths.description: Slavic folklore is rife with tales of vampires and revenants, often depicted as individuals who died unnatural deaths or were buried improperly. These creatures rise from their graves to feed on the living or spread disease, requiring specific rituals and tools to be destroyed.

sources.author: Paul Barber
sources.work: Vampires, Burial, and Death: Folklore and Reality
sources.description: Examines the folklore surrounding vampires and revenants, exploring the cultural anxieties and beliefs that underpin these myths.
sources.text: Examines the folklore surrounding vampires and revenants, exploring the cultural anxieties and beliefs that underpin these myths.

sources.author: Claude Lecouteux
sources.description: Discusses the broader context of nocturnal demons and undead creatures in European folklore, including revenants.
sources.text: Discusses the broader context of nocturnal demons and undead creatures in European folklore, including revenants.

sources.author: Jan Perkowski
sources.work: The Darkling: A Treatise on Slavic Vampirism
sources.description: A detailed exploration of Slavic vampire lore, which often overlaps with beliefs about revenants and other undead creatures.
